description: Figure 3 comprises two scanning electron microscopy (SEM) secondary electron images (20 kV, ×10,000 magnification, 1 μm scale bar) illustrating the microstructural evolution of Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels subjected to heat treatment at 600°C. Subfigure (a) corresponds to the 12Al-10Mn-0.4C alloy (caption reports 2Al-10Mn-0.4C, likely a typographical error for 12Al consistent with the paper context) and reveals a multifaceted microstructure characterized by globular κ-carbide precipitates embedded within carbon-depleted austenite regions, with precipitation concentrated along δ/γ boundaries and within ferrite. Subfigure (b) presents the 12Al-10Mn-1C alloy, displaying well-developed γ+κ-carbide lamellar structures indicative of discontinuous precipitation, featuring alternating lamellae of austenite and κ-carbides. The contrast between the globular precipitation morphology in (a) and the cellular lamellar structure in (b) demonstrates that higher carbon content (1C versus 0.4C) at 12% Al drives a transition from multifaceted precipitation to discontinuous lamellar transformation, which correlates with enhanced hardness (420 HV for 12Al-10Mn-1C) and distinct austenite decomposition kinetics.
